content.text: Can you review the dashboards update data and theses. Come to a final conclusion of 5 tickers to research over the weekend.

I’ll inspect the active dashboard/canvas surfaces first, then run the project’s own status/update commands where they are available so the shortlist is grounded in the vault’s current thesis and signal state. After that I’ll cross-check the candidate tickers for current weekend-relevant context before narrowing to five.

The canvas is an operator map, and the active dashboard files it embeds are the right review surface: signals, freshness, allocation, high-priority theses, full-picture reports, catalyst radar, technical risk, quant, macro, and company risk. `rg` is not installed here, so I’m switching to native PowerShell file enumeration.

The vault API status is usable for the main market workflow: FMP, FRED, SEC, NewsAPI, Treasury, and other core feeds are configured; the missing keys are OSINT-specific. I’m going to refresh the thesis/FMP/catalyst layer now, then inspect the updated thesis frontmatter and latest generated notes.

The FMP watchlist refresh completed across 24 thesis watchlists, 127 unique symbols, and then synced the thesis frontmatter. One data gap showed up: `DRO_AX` technicals failed because the provider returned a premium/invalid response, so I’ll exclude that from any weekend shortlist unless the thesis evidence is strong enough without tape confirmation.

The catalyst layer regenerated 111 symbol notes, conviction delta found only two thesis-level signal changes in the 14-day window, and schema validation passed. I’m applying the scorecard update pass next so the thesis fields line up with the new signal data before I rank tickers.

The ranking signal is not just “highest catalyst score.” I’m weighting: high or strengthening thesis priority, fresh catalyst notes, whether the tape is broken or constructive, valuation/target-upside sanity, and whether the name helps answer a weekend research question rather than just chasing an event.

content.text:
I updated the thesis/FMP/catalyst layer and validation passed. Fresh run covered 24 thesis watchlists, 127 symbols, 111 catalyst notes, and 23 thesis sync updates. Final weekend research list, not a buy list:

1. `VST` — strongest AI-power research candidate. High-priority, high-conviction thesis; monitor is strengthening; earnings due `2026-05-07`; FMP target upside is high, but tape is bearish and below 200DMA. Weekend question: is the technical break noise or thesis damage?

2. `GEV` — best grid-equipment/backlog deep dive. It links AI power, grid bottlenecks, and defense-power stack. Official Q1 showed major order/backlog strength and raised 2026 guidance, but the vault flags it overbought and FMP target upside is negative. Weekend question: does backlog quality justify stretched valuation?

3. `DHI` — housing thesis pressure test. It has the cleanest tape in the homebuilder basket, while the thesis monitor is mixed and break-risk is watch. Official Q2 showed orders up 11%, but affordability remains the key risk. Weekend question: is DHI the right builder exposure, or are cheaper broken-tape peers like `LEN`/`NVR` better research?

4. `KLAC` — semiconductor sovereignty event candidate. High-priority thesis, earnings call on `2026-04-29`, strong quality metrics, but overbought tape and negative target upside. Weekend question: is this an “own quality despite valuation” name or a wait-for-mean-reversion name?

5. `INVA` — best non-megacap asymmetric diversifier. Antimicrobial thesis had the strongest positive conviction delta, the watchlist is technically clean, valuation is reasonable, and Innoviva has real infectious-disease product progress. Weekend question: is this becoming a durable commercial antibiotic platform or still mostly a royalty/investment vehicle?

I would deprioritize `PLTR`, `CEG/SMR/OKLO`, and most defense primes this weekend: they showed high catalyst scores, but mostly because of bearish tape, stale/post-earnings windows, or valuation/event risk rather than clean research setup.

Key sources used: vault FMP watchlists for [AI Power Infrastructure](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Market/2026-04-25_FMP_Thesis_Watchlist_AI_Power_Infrastructure.md>), [Housing](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Market/2026-04-25_FMP_Thesis_Watchlist_Housing_Supply_Correction.md>), [Semis](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Market/2026-04-25_FMP_Thesis_Watchlist_Semiconductor_Sovereignty_&_CHIPS_Act.md>), [AMR](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Market/2026-04-25_FMP_Thesis_Watchlist_Antimicrobial_Resistance_Pipeline.md>), plus official updates from [Vistra](https://investor.vistracorp.com/2026-02-26-Vistra-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2025-Results?asPDF=1), [GE Vernova](https://www.gevernova.com/news/press-releases/ge-vernova-reports-first-quarter-2026-financial), [D.R. Horton](https://investor.drhorton.com/news-and-events/press-releases/2026/04-21-2026-113024627), [KLA](https://ir.kla.com/), and [Innoviva](https://investor.inva.com/news-releases/news-release-details/innoviva-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-financial).
Create a web application that would provide clear data and investment summaries for both theses and investment positions.

I’ll treat this as an in-vault local web app, reusing the existing dashboard/server patterns so it can read thesis notes, pull notes, and position-style frontmatter directly instead of introducing a separate database. I’ll inspect the current dashboard implementation and the Biosecurity thesis note first, then patch the app surface.

The existing structure already has a Node dashboard server, and the opened thesis has rich FMP, monitor, catalyst, and sizing fields. I’m going to extend that local dashboard rather than create a parallel app: add a dedicated investment summary API and a clearer front-end surface for theses plus positions derived from watchlist symbols.

entries.content: Map existing dashboard data flow and note schemas
Add investment summary API from thesis, catalyst, and FMP watchlist notes
Create dedicated web app page for thesis and position summaries
Run validation and browser smoke test

content.text: I’m going to add one server endpoint, `/api/investment-summary`, that composes thesis frontmatter, latest FMP watchlist reports, technical snapshots, earnings rows, and catalyst notes. Then I’ll add `/investments` as a dedicated single-page app so the current operator dashboard remains intact.

content.text: The server patch and app file are in place. I’m checking the Node syntax now, then I’ll start the local dashboard and smoke-test `/investments` plus the JSON endpoint.

content.title: $logDir = Join-Path (Get-Location) '.codex-tmp'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $logDir | Out-Null; $out = Join-Path $logDir 'investment-dashboard.out.log'; $err = Join-Path $logDir 'investment-dashboard.err.log'; $p = Start-Process -FilePath 'node' -ArgumentList @('run.mjs','system','dashboard') -WorkingDirectory (Join-Path (Get-Location) 'scripts') -RedirectStandardOutput $out -RedirectStandardError $err -PassThru; Start-Sleep -Seconds 2; [pscustomobject]@{ Id=$p.Id; Output=$out; Error=$err }

content.text: The server is now running on port 3737. Before I call it done, I’m checking the new JSON endpoint and opening the page in the browser to catch layout or runtime errors.

The first browser pass found the page renders cleanly. One data issue surfaced: I was letting every technical snapshot create a position, which inflated the position count beyond the active thesis watchlists. I’m tightening that so positions are only derived from thesis watchlist symbols, with technical/catalyst notes enriching existing rows.

content.title: $conn = Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 3737 -State Listen -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 1; if ($conn) { Stop-Process -Id $conn.OwningProcess -Force; Start-Sleep -Seconds 1; 'stopped ' + $conn.OwningProcess } else { 'no server' }

content.title: $symbols = @(); Get-ChildItem -File -LiteralPath '10_Theses' -Filter '*.md' | ForEach-Object { $txt = Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $_.FullName; $m=[regex]::Match($txt,'(?m)^fmp_watchlist_symbols:\s*\[(.*)\]'); if($m.Success){ $symbols += [regex]::Matches($m.Groups[1].Value,'"([^"]+)"') | ForEach-Object {$_.Groups[1].Value} } }; $symbols.Count; ($symbols | Sort-Object -Unique).Count

content.text:
Built and started the new investment web app:

http://127.0.0.1:3737/investments

What changed:
- Added `/api/investment-summary` in [server.mjs](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts/dashboard/server.mjs:285>).
- Added the `/investments` route in [server.mjs](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts/dashboard/server.mjs:1003>).
- Added the new single-page app at [investments.html](</C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts/dashboard/investments.html:1>).

The app shows:
- Thesis book summary with priority, conviction, monitor state, primary ticker, tape, and next event.
- Position summaries derived from thesis watchlists, with score, stance, signal status, technical state, valuation, target upside, and catalyst timing.
- Search and filters for `All`, `High`, `Alerts`, and `Events`.
- Detail panes for both thesis summaries and ticker-level investment summaries.

Validation passed:
- `node --check dashboard/server.mjs`
- `node run.mjs system validate`
- Browser smoke-tested desktop and mobile at `/investments`; console is clean.

Current endpoint metrics: `24` theses, `127` thesis-watchlist positions, `60` alert/critical positions, `119` near-term earnings/event rows.
Can you spin up the server
I’ll check whether the dashboard server is already listening on `3737`; if not, I’ll start it and give you the URL.

The server is already running on port `3737`.

Open:
- Investment app: http://127.0.0.1:3737/investments
- Main dashboard: http://127.0.0.1:3737/
Can you remove the learning center button on the main dashboard and add a button to the investment page
I’ll update the main dashboard header so it keeps the research view as the default, removes the Learning Center tab, and adds a direct Investment Summary button that opens `/investments`.

- `Research`
- `Investments` linking to `/investments`

The `Learning Center` button is removed. I reloaded the dashboard and verified the Investments button navigates correctly with no console errors.
